title: Apply 45min timeout to integration tests
body:
IMHO, longer than this waiting for automated testing is "too long".

Scientificaly speaking, based on thousands of runs across many
platforms, successful runs always happen in less time.

If they take longer, something is badly broken.  So in that
case, it's better to fail within a short/defined time, than wait
for the (much longer) automation-level timeout and inevitable
failure.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: I'm wondering if this is actually enough - we've done some work on test speed, but I recall our overall test suite taking around 45 minutes and needing a timeout bump at some point.
Still, LGTM if tests pass consistently

@mheon this is only the e2e timeout, not overall.  But yes it's close, we regularly see 35-38 minutes on Ubuntu, though the Fedoras typically finish around 25-30.  I'm not opposed to making it longer, as having it timeout an otherwise successful run also sucks.  How about 50-minutes, to give some small buffer?

Totally different option:  There's a 'fail fast' option as well, where it will die upon the first failure.  Probably not something we want set on post-merge testing, but we could set that for PR testing.  Do you think people look beyond the first failure when hacking on the code?
